step_by_step_thinking:
Your answer:

I agree with option A. The lesions in rheumatic endocarditis primarily form along the lines of valve closure due to cyclic mechanical stress and immune-mediated injury targeting these areas. These regions experience repetitive shearing forces that promote inflammatory foci and subsequent fibrotic changes characteristic of RHD.
